Nicotiana glauca is a perennial shrub native to Australia, Asia, and parts of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the nightshade family (Solanaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nicotiana glauca include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Nicotiana glauca include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to relieve coughs and respiratory congestion.
